name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [8/8]  We are critical to our country’s success. If you think that is not worth celebrating, I don’t know what is. @SenMikeLee, I invite you to sit down with me if you’d like to learn more about our community and the importance of establishing a National Latino Museum.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [7/8]  Latinos were instrumental in the Civil Rights and Labor Rights movements. We are astronauts and scientists. Teachers and students. Doctors and engineers. We are soldiers and frontline heroes. We are servicemembers and patriots.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [6/8]  Today, there are more than 200,000 Latino active-duty service members serving in the U.S. Armed Forces, & 60 Latino Americans have received the Congressional Medal of Honor, the highest award for valor in action against an enemy force that can be bestowed on an individual.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [5/8]  Latino Americans serve in all branches of the military — like my brother-in-law, who was born in Mexico, served in the U.S. Army, was shot and wounded while fighting in Vietnam — and have bravely fought in every war in American history.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [4/8] Latinos make up 17% — more than 58 mil ppl— of the U.S. population and they are the largest and fastest-growing ethnic group in America. We contribute more than $2.3 tril to the U.S. economy every year; if that were a country’s GDP, it would be the 8th largest in the world.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [3/8]  ...but, more importantly, for its educational purpose. Right now, the Latino community is celebrated and mentioned in less than 1% of the Smithsonian Museums. Hate crimes towards Latinos have skyrocketed in recent years and we’ve been the subject of public disparagement.

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [2/8]  Senator Lee’s comments are precisely why we need a National Latino museum. The Latino community has historically been left out of the conversation and our contributions have always been ignored. We need a Latino Museum not only for its symbolic significance...

name: Rep. Tony Cárdenas
tweet: [1/8] @SenMikeLee, tell me more about how Latinos have been treated and why we are not worth recognition because an overwhelming majority of your congressional colleagues say otherwise.   https://t.co/Cn9V3UtVIq
